data.title: ChatGPT Prompts for E-Commerce Store Owners
data.metaTitle: 15 ChatGPT Prompts for E-Commerce Store Owners (Shopify, WooCommerce) 2026
data.metaDescription: ChatGPT prompts for e-commerce store owners to write product descriptions, email flows, ad copy, and customer service replies. Increase conversions today.
data.keywords: chatgpt prompts for ecommerce, ecommerce product description prompts, shopify ai prompts, chatgpt online store prompts
data.heading: 15 ChatGPT Prompts for E-Commerce Store Owners That Drive Sales
data.intro: E-commerce is a conversion game. Every word on your site, in your emails, and in your ads either moves someone toward a purchase or away from it. These prompts are built to convert.

prompts.title: Product description (benefit-led)
prompts.prompt: Write a product description for [PRODUCT NAME] priced at [PRICE]. Target customer: [AVATAR — e.g., busy mom, outdoor enthusiast]. Key features: [LIST 3-5 FEATURES]. Transform each feature into a customer benefit. Structure: 1-sentence hook, 3-4 sentences of benefits, a bulleted spec list (5 items), and a CTA. Under 150 words main copy. SEO keyword: [KEYWORD].
prompts.tip: Features tell, benefits sell. 'Waterproof' is a feature; 'hike in any weather without worrying' is a benefit.

prompts.title: Abandoned cart email (sequence, email 1)
prompts.prompt: Write the first abandoned cart email for [STORE NAME]. The customer added [PRODUCT NAME] to their cart and didn't checkout. Email 1 (1 hour after abandonment): Short, helpful nudge — mention the product by name, include a link back to the cart, and ask if they had any questions. No discount yet. Under 100 words. Subject line included.
prompts.tip: The first abandoned cart email should not discount. Save the discount for email 3 — most people abandon for non-price reasons.

prompts.title: Product launch email (new arrival)
prompts.prompt: Write a product launch email for [STORE NAME] announcing [PRODUCT NAME]. Include: a subject line that creates intrigue, 1-paragraph story about why this product was created, 3 key benefits, product photo placement note, price, and a CTA button. For email subscribers only — make them feel VIP. Launch offer: [OFFER if any]. Under 250 words.
prompts.tip: Launch emails sent to your subscriber list first (before the general public) create the 'insider' feeling that drives impulse buys.

prompts.title: Facebook/Instagram ad copy (cold audience)
prompts.prompt: Write 3 Facebook/Instagram ad copy variations for [PRODUCT NAME] targeting [COLD AUDIENCE — describe demographics/interests]. Ad objective: [SALES/TRAFFIC/AWARENESS]. Format: Primary text (under 125 chars), Headline (under 27 chars), Description (under 27 chars). Version 1: problem-led, Version 2: social proof-led, Version 3: curiosity hook.
prompts.tip: Cold audience ads that lead with a relatable problem outperform product-feature ads by 3:1.

prompts.title: Customer review request email
prompts.prompt: Write a post-purchase review request email for [STORE NAME], sent [X] days after delivery of [PRODUCT CATEGORY]. Thank the customer for their purchase, ask if everything arrived as expected, and invite them to leave a review with a direct link. Keep it short — the link is the CTA. Under 100 words. Friendly, not transactional.
prompts.tip: Review emails sent 7-14 days after delivery get 4x more responses than those sent at purchase.

prompts.title: Collection page intro copy (SEO)
prompts.prompt: Write a short introduction paragraph (100-150 words) for the top of a [COLLECTION NAME] collection page on [STORE NAME]. Target keyword: '[KEYWORD]'. Include: what this collection is, who it's for, the key benefit, and a sentence mentioning 2-3 product types in the collection. Natural-sounding, SEO-friendly. Avoid keyword stuffing.
prompts.tip: Collection page copy with the target keyword in the first paragraph can move the page 3-5 positions in search results.

prompts.title: Win-back email (lapsed customer)
prompts.prompt: Write a win-back email for [STORE NAME] targeting customers who haven't purchased in [X months]. Include: a subject line that gets reopened ('We miss you' alternatives), a brief note acknowledging the time gap, a compelling reason to come back ([NEW ARRIVALS/LOYALTY CREDITS/SPECIAL OFFER]), and a CTA. Under 150 words.
prompts.tip: Win-back email offers that include a time-limited incentive ('expires in 7 days') have 3x higher conversion rates.

prompts.title: Influencer product seeding DM
prompts.prompt: Write a DM to send to [INFLUENCER TYPE — micro-influencer, niche creator] about sending them a free product from [STORE NAME]. Reference their content specifically ([POST or NICHE]). Explain what you're sending and why it's a fit for their audience. No hard ask — just offer to send it and let them share if they love it. Under 150 words.
prompts.tip: Micro-influencers (10k-100k followers) in a niche have 6x higher engagement than macro-influencers — and are far more accessible.

prompts.title: Post-purchase upsell email
prompts.prompt: Write a post-purchase email for [STORE NAME] sent 2 days after [PRODUCT A] purchase. Suggest complementary product [PRODUCT B] with a brief explanation of why they go together. Include a limited-time discount ([X%]) for the upsell. CTA: Shop now. Under 150 words. Not pushy — helpful framing.
prompts.tip: Post-purchase emails have 5x higher open rates than standard marketing emails. Use that attention for your upsell.

prompts.title: SMS campaign (flash sale)
prompts.prompt: Write a 3-part SMS campaign for a 24-hour flash sale at [STORE NAME]. SMS 1 (launch): Announce the sale, percentage off, link. Under 160 chars. SMS 2 (midday): Tease best sellers still in stock, urgency reminder. Under 160 chars. SMS 3 (2 hours before end): Final warning, countdown language. Under 160 chars.
prompts.tip: SMS flash sales sent to opted-in customers have 25-30% conversion rates — the highest of any marketing channel.

prompts.title: FAQ page answers (reduce support tickets)
prompts.prompt: Write answers to these 5 common customer questions for [STORE NAME]: (1) How long does shipping take? (2) What's your return policy? (3) Are your products [sustainable/vegan/made in X]? (4) Do you ship internationally? (5) How do I track my order? Answers should be clear, specific to our policies ([POLICIES]), and under 75 words each.
prompts.tip: A good FAQ page reduces customer service volume by 25% — the same answers get asked thousands of times.

prompts.title: Product bundle offer copy
prompts.prompt: Write copy for a [PRODUCT BUNDLE NAME] bundle at [STORE NAME] containing [PRODUCT 1], [PRODUCT 2], [PRODUCT 3]. Price: [BUNDLE PRICE] vs. [INDIVIDUAL PRICE]. Include: headline (savings-focused), brief description of each item, and why this combination makes sense for [TARGET CUSTOMER]. Under 150 words.
prompts.tip: Bundles increase average order value by 20-30% when the savings and use-together logic is made clear.

prompts.title: Email subject line split test (5 options)
prompts.prompt: Write 5 email subject line variations for an email promoting [PRODUCT/SALE/OFFER] at [STORE NAME]. Include: 1 curiosity hook, 1 urgency/scarcity, 1 social proof/FOMO, 1 benefit-led, 1 personalization (using first name). Max 45 characters each. No spam trigger words.
prompts.tip: A/B test subject lines on every send. Over 50 emails, optimizing subject lines alone can double open rates.

prompts.title: Homepage hero section copy
prompts.prompt: Write hero section copy for the homepage of [STORE NAME] selling [PRODUCT CATEGORY]. Include: headline (benefit-focused, under 8 words), sub-headline (who it's for + the key outcome, under 20 words), primary CTA button text (under 5 words), and a secondary trust line under the button ([GUARANTEE/REVIEW COUNT/BADGE]). 3 variations.
prompts.tip: Homepage hero headlines should answer one question: 'Why should I buy from you vs. anyone else?' in 8 words or less.

prompts.title: Out-of-stock notification email (back-in-stock)
prompts.prompt: Write a back-in-stock notification email for customers who signed up to be notified about [PRODUCT NAME]. Include: subject line with the product name, 1-sentence excited opener, the product is back (with link), any details on limited quantity, and a strong CTA. Under 100 words. The product sells itself here — keep it short and clear.
prompts.tip: Back-in-stock emails have the highest click-to-purchase conversion rate of any transactional e-commerce email.

data.cta: Automate your store's copywriting with our AI Copy Generator
